_readme: T-1.1 output-snapshot case matrix. Parameters only; the capture logic lives in
tests/snapshot/harness.tsx and the runner in scripts/snapshot/.
Every (station, date) pair here MUST already be covered by the T-1.2 fixture
matrix (tests/fixtures/manifest.json). The harness runs with the network
disabled through the app's own shim (code/ali-je-vroce-era5/fixtures/install.ts),
so an uncovered pair does not silently degrade — it lands in
window.__FIXTURE_MISSES__ and aborts the run.
TWO LOCATIONS PER CASE, because the page has two INDEPENDENT station signals:
today_loc    — the today-card picker      (AliJeVroceERA5.tsx:50, TodayCard.tsx:111)
analysis_loc — the trend/analysis picker  (RegressionPanel.tsx:37, synced from
the map at AliJeVroceERA5.tsx:126)
Selecting a station in the today card does NOT move the analysis panel and vice
versa, so collapsing them into one field would snapshot a page state that cannot
occur. On a default page load today_loc is the national average while analysis_loc
is already Ljubljana (fetchMeta default_location, api.ts:319) — that is case
`national-primary` below, and it is the literal default page.
FIXTURES ARE VALID ONLY WITHIN A DATA GENERATION (CLAUDE.md). Every number this
matrix pins encodes stage's committed-CSV data as of 2026-07-22. The D-4/D-5
regeneration invalidates all of it and Phase 4 must re-record and re-baseline
deliberately.

_note: The analysis panel's own controls, at the values a default page load uses.
Held fixed across every case so a case diff is attributable to (station, date)
and nothing else. doy is NOT here — it is derived per case from the selected

cases.purpose: THE DEFAULT PAGE LOAD, verbatim. Pinned 'today' sits inside the ERA5-Land reanalysis lag, so climate-si has no `daily` row and fetchTodayStatus falls through to the live Open-Meteo national average (api.ts:362-366) and marks the result preliminary — this is the case that pins the 'ERA5T · preliminarno' badge string (TodayCard.tsx:183), known inaccurate per D-11 and snapshotted as-is.
Lowland station on the preliminary branch: per-station Open-Meteo forecast downscaled to the station elevation (api.ts:466-468). The per-station counterpart to national-primary.
Altitude station (D-5) on the preliminary branch. Kredarica is presented as an ERA5-Land grid cell at 1307 m; T-4.4 removes its lapse correction and this case is where that shows up first.

National average on the SETTLED branch: far enough back that the reanalysis is final, so the datasette daily rows are averaged client-side (api.ts:354-360) and Open-Meteo is never called. No preliminary badge. The control for national-primary.
Lowland station, datasette-authoritative branch (api.ts:458-462). Control for ljubljana-primary; the pair isolates the preliminary/Open-Meteo path from everything else.
Altitude station, datasette-authoritative branch. Control for kredarica-primary.

LEAP DAY, and a documented current-behaviour defect rather than a healthy case. month=2 day=29 is absent from every month/day-keyed precomputed table, so daily_window returns zero rows, fetchTodayStatus returns {available:false} (api.ts:473) and the entire today section collapses; fetchAnnualTrend THROWS 'No annual trend row' (api.ts:1433) rather than degrading. Snapshotted as-is. NOT FIXED HERE — this is T-4.5.
The same leap-day collapse at the altitude station, to show it is a calendar defect and not a per-station data gap.

The same leap-day collapse on the national path, which reaches it by a different route: the national daily_window query (api.ts:214-216) returns zero rows and fetchTodayStatus bails at api.ts:352, before any temperature lookup.

Mar 1 IN A LEAP YEAR. monthDayToDoy (api.ts:107-110) reads a fixed non-leap day table, so this is where a leap-year doy off-by-one first becomes visible in a rendered number. Pairs with ljubljana-nonleap-mar01.

Last day of February in a NON-leap year. The 2024 pair alone only proves the Feb 29 -> Mar 1 transition; this shows what Feb 28 -> Mar 1 does with no Feb 29 in between. Its 7-day lookback (api.ts:553-557) crosses Feb 28 rather than Feb 29.

Mar 1 in a non-leap year. Same month/day as ljubljana-leap-mar01 — so the month/day-keyed climatology is shared — but a different date, so the date-keyed today temperature and the last-7 strip differ. Any divergence beyond those two is a leap-year bug.
Year boundary, low end. The +/-7-day climatology window (api.ts:56-61) and the +/-30-day trend window must wrap across the year end; Jan 1 is where wrap-around arithmetic breaks.
Year boundary, high end. Same wrap-around exposure from the other side; the last-7-days strip also crosses into the previous year.
Above p95 at the lowland station — pins the 'hell' branch of categorizeEra5 (api.ts:140-153) and the top distribution zone. August 2013 was a documented Slovenian heat episode.
Above p95 at the ALTITUDE station on the same day, so one date exercises the hot extreme at both elevations. Confirms the extreme is a synoptic event and not a lowland artefact.

Below p5 at the lowland station — pins the 'freezing' branch and the bottom distribution zone. LJUBLJANA ONLY: the February 2012 European cold wave was a lowland event in this dataset, and the same date at Kredarica is an ordinary early-February day (see the counter-check below). The altitude p5 case is kredarica-p5.

counter_check._note: Recorded so nobody re-derives this the hard way: the same date at Kredarica is NOT a p5 case and must not be used as one.
counter_check.verdict: -14.707 > p5 -15.97 — INSIDE the band, an ordinary early-February day at 1307 m

cases.purpose: Below p5 at the ALTITUDE station, and the reason 2012-02-07 alone is not enough (see ljubljana-p5 counter_check). January 1985 was a documented central-European cold wave. Below p5 at BOTH stations, so it also doubles as a lowland cold control. NOT a record claim: the committed fixtures hold this date and the +/-7-day climatology window, not the full daily series, so nothing here can rank the day against Kredarica's whole record.
